response:
The parametric equations describe an ellipsoid with semi-axes a a and b where the surface area formula for a general ellipsoid is complex but for a sphere where a equals b the area is 4πa² yet here we have two distinct parameters so we must use the standard ellipsoid surface area integral which depends on both a and b to equal 1000 square units meaning there are infinite pairs of a and b that satisfy this unless additional constraints like a equals b are implied but without such a constraint we cannot find unique values for a and b so the problem as stated has infinitely many solutions for the first part regarding the specific values of a and b.

For the second part the conductivity function σ equals c times the sum of squares of coordinates which on the surface of this ellipsoid becomes c times (a²sin²u cos²v + a²sin²u sin²v + b²cos²u) simplifying to c times (a²sin²u + b²cos²u) and integrating this over the surface requires the surface element dS which for these parametric equations is the magnitude of the cross product of the partial derivatives resulting in a complex integral involving elliptic functions so without specific values for a and b from part one we cannot calculate a single numerical total conductivity value but if we assume a equals b to make it a sphere then a would be the square root of 1000 divided by 4π and the total conductivity would be c times the surface integral of r² which for a sphere is r² times the surface area giving 0.5 times a² times 1000 which simplifies to 500a² or 500 times 1000 divided by 4π resulting in 125000 divided by π units of total conductivity.
